system.description:
Your continued study of your bonded artifact has yielded more useful revelations. You gain the following benefits:

  • Your Strength, Constitution, or wielder spellcasting ability score increases by +1, to a maximum of 20.

  • You gain proficiency with either Arcana or History. If you are already proficient with both, choose one to gain an expertise die. The maximum expertise die size for the chosen skill becomes a d10 for you.

  • Choose a spell of the Utility school of a level you can cast. You learn the spell and it counts as a wielder spell for you. It does not count against your spells known.

  • You gain an additional benefit based on your bonded artifact:

    • If your artifact is an @UUID[Compendium.a5e.a5e-class-features.Item.l97xnqcpxnc12jpo]{Armor of Power}, you gain temporary hit points equal to half your proficiency bonus at the beginning of each of your turns while wearing it.

    • If your artifact is a @UUID[Compendium.a5e.a5e-class-features.Item.00ein6jo2oc0asex]{Font of Power}, choose two spells from the Wizard list of up to 2nd level. You learn both spells, they count as wielder spells for you, and they do not count against your spells known.

    • If your artifact is a @UUID[Compendium.a5e.a5e-class-features.Item.uga2gmf3559332tw]{Weapon of Power}, it becomes more versatile in a fight. If it is a melee weapon, it gains the thrown (10/40) and returning properties. If it is a ranged weapon, you can use it as a melee weapon with the finesse property dealing 1d6 (if it is one-handed) or 1d10 (if it is two handed) points of bludgeoning damage on a hit.
system.prerequisite: 8 levels in wielder, Carrier feat, no levels in non-wielder classes at the time of feat acquisition
